text_rules: Use max 3-4 Hinglish words with a specific ₹ amount or number to create instant curiosity — pair a loss/gain figure with an emotional trigger word like Trap, Mistake, or Destroyed. Numbers (₹40K, 500 T-Shirts, 3X) always outperform vague claims; odd specificity (₹12 Thread, ₹6 Tag) stops the scroll harder than round numbers.
layout: Place the person/product on the left or right third of the frame with bold hook text on the opposite side, all text strictly within the 25%-70% vertical band to survive Instagram grid-crop and YouTube's bottom UI overlay. Use a dark or contrasting background behind text — never place text over busy visuals — and keep font size large enough to read on a 4-inch phone preview.
patterns: The highest-performing Indian business Reels thumbnails follow a 'Specific Loss + Hidden Cause' formula — a concrete rupee amount lost paired with a surprising everyday detail (₹12 thread, ₹6 tag, GSM number) that the viewer didn't know to check. Faces showing shock, disappointment, or disbelief dramatically increase stop-rate; holding the actual product (ruined t-shirt, cheap thread) adds proof and context. Red/yellow bold text on dark backgrounds with a 💀 or 🤯 emoji as a visual anchor consistently outperforms clean, polished thumbnails in this niche.
ig_patterns: High save-rate thumbnails promise reusable knowledge — checklist-style hooks ('Check These 3 Things'), comparison frameworks (A vs B vs C), and quality-test methods that buyers can reference repeatedly before purchasing. High share-rate thumbnails trigger 'my friend is making this exact mistake' — they name a specific, common error with a painful ₹ consequence that feels personally urgent to forward. Combining both: use a specific cost-of-mistake number (₹40K, 1000 returns) with an actionable but incomplete reveal (the '3 things' or 'one trap') so viewers save for later AND tag their reseller friends.
